channel.title: NHC Atlantic
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Atlantic, Caribbean, and the Gulf of America

item.title: Atlantic Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
023
ABNT20 KNHC 200552
TWOAT

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
200 AM EDT Mon Jul 20 2026

For the North Atlantic...Caribbean Sea and the Gulf of America:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Depression Two, located over the northeastern Gulf of America.

Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

&&
Public Advisories on Tropical Depression Two are issued
under WMO header WTNT32 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCPAT2.
Forecast/Advisories on Tropical Depression Two are issued
under WMO header WTNT22 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCMAT2.

$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Depression Two (AT2/AL022026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...DEPRESSION CONTINUES DRIFTING NORTH-NORTHWESTWARD... As of 1:00 AM CDT Mon Jul 20 the center of Two was located near 28.4, -85.3 with movement NNW at 3 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1007 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 30 mph.

item.title: Tropical Depression Two Public Advisory Number 3a
item.description:
Issued at 100 AM CDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTNT32 KNHC 200556
TCPAT2
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Depression Two Intermediate Advisory Number 3A
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
100 AM CDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
...DEPRESSION CONTINUES DRIFTING NORTH-NORTHWESTWARD...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 100 AM CDT...0600 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...28.4N 85.3W
ABOUT 125 MI...205 KM S OF PANAMA CITY FLORIDA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...30 MPH...45 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NNW OR 335 DEGREES AT 3 MPH...6 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1007 MB...29.74 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
CHANGES WITH THIS ADVISORY:
 
None.
 
SUMMARY OF WATCHES AND WARNINGS IN EFFECT:
 
A Tropical Storm Watch is in effect for...
* Ochlockonee River to the FL/AL border
 
A Tropical Storm Watch means that tropical storm conditions are
possible within the watch area, in this case in the next 36 to 48
hours.
 
Additional tropical storm watches or warnings will likely be
required over the next day or two.
 
For storm information specific to your area, including possible
inland watches and warnings, please monitor products issued by your
local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 100 AM CDT (0600 UTC), the center of Tropical Depression Two was 
located by aircraft reconnaissance near latitude 28.4 North, 
longitude 85.3 West. The depression is moving toward the 
north-northwest near 3 mph (6 km/h). A slow northwestward or 
north-northwestward motion is expected during the next day or so, 
followed by a turn toward the west-northwest or west. On the 
forecast track, the depression will move near or along the northern 
Gulf coast during the next several days.

Maximum sustained winds are near 30 mph (45 km/h) with higher gusts. 
Gradual strengthening is forecast, and the depression is expected to 
become a tropical storm later today.
 
The minimum central pressure based on data from an Air Force Reserve 
reconnaissance aircraft is 1007 mb (29.74 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
Key messages for Tropical Depression Two can be found in the
Tropical Cyclone Discussion under AWIPS header MIATCDAT2 and WMO
header WTNT42 KNHC.
 
WIND:  Tropical storm conditions are possible within the watch
area by late today.
 
STORM SURGE:  The combination of storm surge and the tide will
cause normally dry areas near the immediate coast to be flooded by
rising waters moving inland from the shoreline.  The water could
reach the following heights above ground somewhere in the indicated
areas if the peak surge occurs at the time of high tide...
 
Indian Pass to Chassahowitzka, FL...1 to 3 ft
 
The deepest water will occur along the immediate coast.
Surge-related flooding depends on the relative timing of the surge
and the tidal cycle, and can vary greatly over short distances.  For
information specific to your area, please see products issued by
your local National Weather Service forecast office.
 
For a complete depiction of areas at risk of storm surge
inundation, please see the National Weather Service Peak
Storm Surge Graphic, available at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?peakSurge.
 
RAINFALL: The tropical depression is expected to produce rainfall
amounts of 2 to 4 inches with locally higher totals around 8 inches
through Friday along the Gulf Coast and adjacent inland locations
from western Florida to the Middle Texas Coast, including southern
portions of Alabama, Mississippi, and Louisiana. This rainfall may
produce areas of flash flooding, especially in urban areas.
 
For a complete depiction of forecast rainfall associated with
Tropical Depression Two, please see the National Weather Service
Storm Total Rainfall Graphic available at
hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?rainqpf and the Flash Flood Risk
graphic at hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ero.
 
SURF:  Swells generated by the depression are affecting portions of
the Gulf coast of Florida and are likely to spread westward during
the week.  These swells are likely to cause life-threatening surf
and rip current conditions.  Please consult products from your
local weather office.
 
A depiction of rip current risk for the United States can be found
at: hurricanes.gov/graphics_at2.shtml?ripCurrents
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 400 AM CDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Papin
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Depression Two Forecast Advisory Number 3
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
WTNT22 KNHC 200244
TCMAT2
 
TROPICAL DEPRESSION TWO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER   3
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026
0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
TROPICAL DEPRESSION CENTER LOCATED NEAR 28.0N  85.1W AT 20/0300Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTH-NORTHWEST OR 335 DEGREES AT   3 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1008 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  25 KT WITH GUSTS TO  35 KT.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 28.0N  85.1W AT 20/0300Z
AT 20/0000Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 27.7N  85.0W
 
FORECAST VALID 20/1200Z 28.3N  85.3W
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 28.6N  85.7W
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 50NE  50SE   0SW   0NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 28.8N  86.2W
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 60NE  50SE  30SW  40NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 29.1N  87.0W
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  50NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 29.3N  88.1W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  50NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z 29.2N  89.6W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  50NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 125 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 175 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 24/0000Z 29.0N  93.5W
MAX WIND  50 KT...GUSTS  60 KT.
50 KT... 30NE  20SE   0SW  30NW.
34 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  50NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/0000Z 28.8N  97.8W...INLAND
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 28.0N  85.1W
 
INTERMEDIATE PUBLIC ADVISORY...WTNT32 KNHC/MIATCPAT2...AT 20/0600Z
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/0900Z
 
$$
FORECASTER BLAKE
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Depression Two Forecast Discussion Number 3
item.description:
Issued at 1000 PM CDT Sun Jul 19 2026 
934 
WTNT42 KNHC 200245
TCDAT2
 
Tropical Depression Two Discussion Number   3
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       AL022026
1000 PM CDT Sun Jul 19 2026

The depression is producing a small area of deep convection near 
and north of the apparent center, with other modest bands mostly in 
the eastern semicircle.  Since the various satellite intensity 
estimates are unchanged and the nearby surface observations haven't 
reported much more than 20 kt, the initial intensity will remain 
25 kt for this advisory.  This is also consistent with a 
just-received partial scatterometer pass.

The system is drifting to the north-northwest, caught in light 
steering between a ridge over the western Atlantic and one over the 
south-central United States.  Most of the guidance suggests that 
this slow motion, with some turn to the west-northwest, will 
continue early this week, and the cyclone's outer bands could bring 
tropical storm conditions to portions of the Florida Panhandle 
beginning late Monday.  A turn to the west and an increase in 
forward speed are forecast late on Tuesday as the ridge over the 
U.S. builds eastward, causing the system to move along or just 
offshore of the northwestern Gulf coast during the middle and latter 
portions of the week.  The track has been shifted a bit to the north 
in the short-term, blending back with the previous forecast on day 
2, then a bit to the south at long-term.  This is consistent with 
the latest model consensus and Google DeepMind solutions.

Only slow strengthening of the system is expected due to its broad 
structure, pockets of mid-level dry air, and northeasterly vertical 
wind shear, despite very warm waters.  The cyclone is still 
forecast to become a tropical storm on Monday when it is offshore of 
the Florida Panhandle.  An increase in shear is expected by 
Tuesday, which could cause the intensity to flat line, along with 
some land interaction.  This is a challenging forecast because of 
the expected moderate shear conditions, which are known to result 
in less predictable outcomes. For instance, reliable models are 
spread from near dissipation by the ECMWF/HAFS guidance but near 
hurricane-strength in the latest HWRF/HMON runs.  The NHC forecast 
continues to lie between those solutions, but it should be mentioned 
that I don't have much confidence in this prediction given the above 
factors.

 
Key Messages:
 
1. The depression is expected to gradually strengthen and
could bring tropical storm conditions to portions of the Florida
Panhandle beginning late Monday, where a Tropical Storm Watch is in
effect.
 
2. Interests in Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Texas should
monitor the progress of this system, as watches could be required
for portions of that area over the next day or two.
 
3. Areas of flash flooding will be possible through Thursday along
the eastern and central Gulf Coast from western Florida to southern
portions of Alabama, Mississippi, and Louisiana.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/0300Z 28.0N  85.1W   25 KT  30 MPH
 12H  20/1200Z 28.3N  85.3W   30 KT  35 MPH
 24H  21/0000Z 28.6N  85.7W   35 KT  40 MPH
 36H  21/1200Z 28.8N  86.2W   40 KT  45 MPH
 48H  22/0000Z 29.1N  87.0W   40 KT  45 MPH
 60H  22/1200Z 29.3N  88.1W   45 KT  50 MPH
 72H  23/0000Z 29.2N  89.6W   45 KT  50 MPH
 96H  24/0000Z 29.0N  93.5W   50 KT  60 MPH
120H  25/0000Z 28.8N  97.8W   30 KT  35 MPH...INLAND
 
$$
Forecaster Blake
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Depression Two Wind Speed Probabilities Number 3
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
FONT12 KNHC 200244
PWSAT2
                                                                    
TROPICAL DEPRESSION TWO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER   3         
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       AL022026               
0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0300Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL DEPRESSION TWO WAS LOCATED NEAR     
LATITUDE 28.0 NORTH...LONGITUDE 85.1 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED    
WINDS NEAR 25 KTS...30 MPH...45 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   ATLANTIC STANDARD TIME (AST)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   EASTERN  DAYLIGHT TIME (EDT)...SUBTRACT 4 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   CENTRAL  DAYLIGHT TIME (CDT)...SUBTRACT 5 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        00Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       00Z MON 12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI 00Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
APALACHICOLA   34  1   6( 7)   6(13)   2(15)   2(17)   X(17)   X(17)
 
GFAM 290N 850W 34  9  13(22)   8(30)   1(31)   1(32)   X(32)   1(33)
GFAM 290N 850W 50  X   1( 1)   2( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
PANAMA CITY FL 34  X   5( 5)   7(12)   4(16)   4(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
DESTIN EXEC AP 34  X   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)   2( 5)   X( 5)   1( 6)
 
WHITING FLD FL 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)   1( 4)   X( 4)
 
PENSACOLA FL   34  X   X( X)   1( 1)   1( 2)   3( 5)   1( 6)   X( 6)
 
GFAM 290N 870W 34  X   7( 7)  18(25)  12(37)   9(46)   1(47)   X(47)
GFAM 290N 870W 50  X   X( X)   2( 2)   4( 6)   3( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)
GFAM 290N 870W 64  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   1( 2)   X( 2)   X( 2)
 
MOBILE AL      34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   4( 5)   1( 6)   X( 6)
 
GULFPORT MS    34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)   2( 7)   X( 7)
 
STENNIS MS     34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   2( 8)   X( 8)
 
BURAS LA       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)   4(13)   1(14)
 
GFAM 280N 890W 34  X   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)  16(19)   3(22)   1(23)
GFAM 280N 890W 50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   2( 5)   X( 5)
 
NEW ORLEANS LA 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   4( 7)   X( 7)
 
HOUMA LA       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   6(10)   1(11)

BATON ROUGE LA 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   3( 4)   X( 4)
 
MORGAN CITY LA 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   6( 8)   1( 9)
 
LAFAYETTE LA   34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   3( 4)   1( 5)
 
NEW IBERIA LA  34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   4( 5)   1( 6)

GFAM 280N 930W 50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   1( 5)
GFAM 280N 930W 64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)

GALVESTON TX   50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   2( 4)

GFAM 280N 950W 50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   2( 4)
 
HIGH ISLAND TX 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   3( 7)
 
MATAGORDA TX   34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   7( 7)   8(15)
MATAGORDA TX   50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   3( 4)
 
PORT O CONNOR  34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)   9(14)
PORT O CONNOR  50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)
 
ROCKPORT TX    34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   4( 5)
 
CORPUS CHRISTI 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
GFAM 270N 960W 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   8(12)
GFAM 270N 960W 50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
GFAM 250N 960W 34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER BLAKE
